notes: Theosis is participation in God's uncreated energies (Palamite essence/energies distinction), not the Roman Catholic created-grace model of sanctification nor the Lutheran forensic justification, so the traditions must stay separate.
provenance: Gregory Palamas, Triads; affirmed at the Hesychast Councils of Constantinople (1341-1351).

canonicalTitleEn: Roman Catholic Filioque (procession of the Spirit from Father and Son)
notes: The Latin addition that the Spirit proceeds 'and from the Son' is precisely what Eastern Orthodoxy rejects as uncanonical, so the two pneumatologies are distinct rather than interchangeable.
provenance: Filioque inserted in Western recensions of the Nicene-Constantinopolitan Creed; defended at the Council of Florence (1439).

canonicalTitleEn: Oriental Orthodox miaphysitism (one united nature of Christ)
notes: Miaphysitism affirms one incarnate nature 'out of two' and rejects the Chalcedonian two-natures formula, yet is equally opposed to Nestorian division, so it is a third distinct christology, not a variant of either.
provenance: Cyril of Alexandria's 'mia physis tou theou logou sesarkomene'; Coptic, Syriac and Armenian churches' rejection of the Council of Chalcedon (451).

canonicalTitleEn: Church of the East dyophysite christology (two qnome)
notes: The East Syriac tradition confesses two natures and two qnome in Christ, a framework distinct from both Chalcedonian hypostatic union and Oriental Orthodox miaphysitism, and should not be flattened into 'Nestorianism' as a slur.
provenance: Synod of Mar Aba I and the East Syriac liturgical/theological tradition; christology articulated by Babai the Great (c. 600).

canonicalTitleEn: Lutheran sacramental union (real presence in/with/under bread and wine)
notes: Lutheran sacramental union holds Christ's body truly present 'in, with, and under' the elements without transubstantiation, distinct from the Reformed spiritual/pneumatic presence and from the Catholic conversion of substance.
provenance: Augsburg Confession Art. X and the Formula of Concord (1577), against both Zwinglian and Roman views.

canonicalTitleEn: Reformed pneumatic presence in the Lord's Supper
notes: Calvin's view that believers are lifted by the Spirit to feed on the ascended Christ is distinct from Lutheran local real presence and from Zwingli's bare-memorialism, so 'Reformed' must not be merged with either.
provenance: John Calvin, Institutes IV.17; codified in the Westminster Confession and Heidelberg Catechism.

notes: Ramanuja's Vishishtadvaita treats souls and matter as the real body/modes (sharira) of Brahman, distinct from Vallabha's Shuddhadvaita pure-monism and from Bhedabheda's simultaneous difference-and-nondifference.
provenance: Ramanuja, Sri Bhashya commentary on the Brahma Sutras (12th c.).

notes: Vallabha's Shuddhadvaita holds the world is real and non-illusory while souls are identical-in-essence with Brahman without qualification, differing from Ramanuja's qualified scheme and from Chaitanya's inconceivable difference-nondifference.
provenance: Vallabha, Anubhashya and Shodasha Granthah; Pushtimarg tradition (16th c.).

canonicalTitleEn: Achintya Bheda Abheda (inconceivable difference and non-difference, Chaitanya)
notes: The Gaudiya doctrine that the soul is simultaneously and inconceivably one with and different from Bhagavan is a distinct Vedantic position, not reducible to Ramanuja's or Vallabha's systems.
provenance: Jiva Goswami, Sat Sandarbhas; Gaudiya Vaishnava tradition stemming from Chaitanya Mahaprabhu (16th c.).

notes: Shinran's Jodo Shinshu makes salvation wholly Amida's Other-Power received as entrusting faith (shinjin), distinct from Honen's Jodo-shu emphasis on repeated nembutsu practice and from Tendai's self-power meditative path.
provenance: Shinran, Kyogyoshinsho (13th c.); foundational Jodo Shinshu scripture.

canonicalTitleEn: Druze Tawhid (Unitarian) faith and the seven luminaries
notes: The Druze movement (al-Muwahhidun) emerged from Fatimid Ismailism but developed a closed, distinct cosmology including reincarnation and the divine manifestation in al-Hakim, and is not a sect of Twelver Shia Islam.
provenance: Rasa'il al-Hikma (Epistles of Wisdom) compiled by Hamza ibn Ali and Baha al-Din al-Muqtana (11th c.).

canonicalTitleEn: Baha'i progressive revelation and the unity of religion
notes: The Baha'i Faith teaches successive Manifestations of God in an unending cycle and regards Baha'u'llah as a new independent revelation, distinct from the Islamic seal-of-prophethood framework it grew out of and from its precursor Babism.

canonicalTitleEn: LDS exaltation and the social Godhead of three beings
notes: The LDS (Mormon) teaching of exaltation toward godhood and a Godhead of three distinct beings is materially different from creedal Trinitarian Christianity's one-substance God, and differs again from the Community of Christ's more traditional theology.
provenance: Joseph Smith, King Follett Discourse (1844); Doctrine and Covenants and the Articles of Faith.
